description: Alerting rules for the Data & Analytics Pipeline chained event scenario

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] Data Pipeline — High Failure Rate
rules.investigationGuide:
# High Failure Rate

The orchestrator is logging more failures than normal.

## Triage
1. Are failures on one DAG? Run: `FROM logs-aws.mwaa-* | WHERE event.outcome == "failure" | STATS failures = COUNT(*) BY aws.mwaa.dag_id | SORT failures DESC | LIMIT 10`
2. Check linked dashboard for the spike shape
3. Check if EMR/Spark or S3 rules also fired

## Escalate when
- Multiple DAGs failing
- Failures still rising after 15 min
- SLA-bound data affected

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] Data Pipeline — Null/Empty Data Detected
rules.investigationGuide:
# Null/Empty Data Detected

A query returned zero rows — the upstream load may have silently failed.

## Triage
1. Identify the empty query: `FROM logs-aws.athena-* | WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1h AND aws.athena.rows_returned == 0 | KEEP @timestamp, aws.athena.query_id, aws.athena.workgroup | SORT @timestamp DESC | LIMIT 25`
2. Check the source partition exists
3. If orchestrator shows "success" but query is empty — silent data loss

## Escalate when
- Query feeds an SLA-bound dataset
- Empty for two consecutive runs

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] Data Pipeline — EMR/Spark Processing Error
rules.investigationGuide:
# EMR/Spark Processing Error

The compute cluster is logging exceptions.

## Triage
1. Classify errors: `FROM logs-aws.emr-* | WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1h AND error.type IS NOT NULL | STATS errors = COUNT(*) BY error.type, host.id | SORT errors DESC | LIMIT 20`
2. Same host failing = infra; many hosts same error = code/data
3. Check if High Failure Rate also fired

## Escalate when
- Multiple clusters affected
- Same error after cluster restart

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] Data Pipeline — S3 Source File Format Error
rules.investigationGuide:
# S3 Source File Format Error

An unexpected file format landed in the source bucket.

## Triage
1. List bad files: `FROM logs-aws.s3access-* | WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1h | WHERE NOT ENDS_WITH(aws.s3access.key, ".avro") OR aws.s3access.key LIKE "*%*" | KEEP @timestamp, aws.s3access.key, aws.s3access.bucket_name | SORT @timestamp DESC | LIMIT 50`
2. Check if one producer or many
3. Move bad file to quarantine prefix

## Escalate when
- Multiple producers writing wrong format
- Bucket versioning overwritten

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] Data Pipeline — Slow Pipeline Run (>60s)
rules.investigationGuide:
# Slow Pipeline Run (>60s)

A DAG exceeded its informal SLA.

## Triage
1. Compare to baseline: `FROM logs-aws.mwaa-* | WHERE event.action == "dag_completed" AND @timestamp > NOW() - 7d | STATS p50 = PERCENTILE(aws.mwaa.duration_ms, 50), p95 = PERCENTILE(aws.mwaa.duration_ms, 95) BY aws.mwaa.dag_id | WHERE p95 > 30000 | SORT p95 DESC`
2. If within 2x of p95, likely noise
3. Check EMR errors and input volume

## Escalate when
- SLA-bound DAG slow for two consecutive runs
- Correlated with executor errors

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] Data Pipeline — Glue Schema Drift Detected
rules.investigationGuide:
# Glue Schema Drift Detected

The Glue Crawler detected schema changes (columns added, removed, or type-changed) in a catalogued table. Downstream Athena queries may fail.

## Triage
1. Check drift details: `FROM logs-aws.glue* | WHERE aws.glue.schema_change_type IS NOT NULL AND @timestamp > NOW() - 1h | KEEP @timestamp, aws.glue.crawler_name, aws.glue.database_name, aws.glue.schema_change_type, aws.glue.schema_change_column | SORT @timestamp DESC`
2. Check for Athena failures caused by the drift: `FROM logs-aws.athena* | WHERE event.outcome == "failure" AND error.type == "COLUMN_NOT_FOUND" AND @timestamp > NOW() - 1h | KEEP @timestamp, aws.athena.workgroup, aws.athena.database, error.message`
3. Review the CloudTrail UpdateTable event for the exact schema change
4. If the change is expected (new upstream column), update downstream queries
5. If unexpected, investigate the source data for corruption or upstream pipeline changes

## Escalate when
- Multiple Athena queries fail due to the drift
- The schema change was not part of a planned release
- TYPE_CHANGED drift (breaking change for existing queries)
